{"description":"src/cli/ has 76 bare print() calls across 4 files, bypassing the View protocol:
- cmd_system.py: 25 violations (cmd_audit, cmd_fs_ls, status reporting)
- cmd_audio.py: 13 violations (cmd_optimize progress, optimize-all summary, fingerprint output)
- cmd_slots.py: 11 violations (squash reporting, move/copy progress, blank separators)
- display.py: 27 violations (TerminalView rendering — these may be intentional since TerminalView IS the stdout renderer, but should still go through a controlled output path)

These break --quiet and --json modes. All output should go through view.info()/view.step()/view.kv()/view.progress() methods.

Priority order: cmd_system.py (most violations, untestable), cmd_audio.py (user-facing progress), cmd_slots.py (operation feedback).

Note: Core layer prints are tracked separately in ko2-tools-kzh (ARCH-011).

Evidence: Grep for '^\s+print\(' across src/cli/ returns 76 matches."}

Resolved: all upload operations now use opcode 0x7E (UPLOAD), matching the official TE app. Confirmed from captures/sniffer-upload21.jsonl and verified on real device with roundtrip test. PROTOCOL.md updated. The device also accepts 0x6C but 0x7E is canonical.

{"notes":"CONFIRMED from capture tui-2026-03-21-210118:
- User selected 3 files at 21:06:01
- Waveform precalc continued downloading for 9+ minutes (21:06 to 21:15)
- Upload request was NEVER processed — zero "Uploaded" or "Running upload" messages
- App had to be force-killed

The _maybe_run_waveform_precalc_step check `if not self._request_queue.empty(): return` 
runs once per slot before each download. Downloads block 5-10s each. With 405 slots, 
precalc dominates the worker thread for 30+ minutes.

Root cause needs deeper investigation — the queue check SHOULD abort precalc when 
request arrives. Either Queue.empty() is unreliable across threads, or the request 
is submitted after the check but the next iteration somehow misses it again.

Immediate fix: precalc must be interruptible. Either check the queue MID-download 
(inside _download_data loop), or run precalc in a separate thread that can be cancelled."}
old_value: {"id":"ko2-tools-36z","title":"TUI: typing in rename popup lags due to waveform precalc","description":"Waveform precalc downloads run on the same worker thread. Even though the worker checks queue emptiness before starting a precalc step, once a download is in progress it blocks the thread. Any keypress that triggers a worker request (or state update) gets delayed. The rename input visibly lags. Fix: either pause waveform precalc while modals are open, or move precalc to a separate thread/process.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"ivan123.public@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-22T01:00:42Z","created_by":"Ivan Cherniukh","updated_at":"2026-03-22T01:00:42Z"}

new_value:
{"notes":"ADDITIONAL FINDINGS from tui-2026-03-21-210118.jsonl:

48 waveform downloads attempted. Total time: 13.6 minutes.
Download durations degrade over time: first downloads 1-4s, later ones 17s, 24s, 38s, 
62s, 98s, and one at 182s (3 minutes for single slot — 8047 GET_DATA pages, ~3.5MB sample).

Root causes:
1. Precalc downloads ALL slots including huge samples — no size limit or priority
2. Downloads not cancellable mid-transfer — blocks worker for minutes per large sample
3. No mechanism to interrupt precalc when user queues an operation
4. _initialize() called 14 times during large download window (hardcoded timeout overhead)

Fix priorities:
- P0: Make downloads cancellable (check queue between pages)
- P0: Skip precalc immediately when user request arrives
- P1: Size-limit precalc downloads (skip samples > N bytes)
- P1: Prioritize visible/selected slots over bulk precalc"}

- User selected 3 files at 21:06:01
- Waveform precalc continued downloading for 9+ minutes (21:06 to 21:15)
- Upload request was NEVER processed — zero "Uploaded" or "Running upload" messages
- App had to be force-killed

The _maybe_run_waveform_precalc_step check `if not self._request_queue.empty(): return` 
runs once per slot before each download. Downloads block 5-10s each. With 405 slots, 
precalc dominates the worker thread for 30+ minutes.

Root cause needs deeper investigation — the queue check SHOULD abort precalc when 
request arrives. Either Queue.empty() is unreliable across threads, or the request 
is submitted after the check but the next iteration somehow misses it again.

Immediate fix: precalc must be interruptible. Either check the queue MID-download 
(inside _download_data loop), or run precalc in a separate thread that can be cancelled.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"ivan123.public@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-22T01:00:42Z","created_by":"Ivan Cherniukh","updated_at":"2026-03-22T01:19:23Z"}

{"description":"Observed in capture tui-2026-03-21-210118.jsonl:

Downloads 1-31: 230-400 pages/s
Download 32 (during/after file picker): 113 pages/s  
Downloads 33-47: 24-37 pages/s

Throughput dropped 10x and did not recover for the rest of the session.
The drop coincides with the yazi file picker (app.suspend/resume) but
causation is not established — could be device state, MIDI driver,
accumulated protocol errors, or something else.

Needs investigation: reproduce with and without file picker to isolate cause.","title":"TUI: MIDI throughput drops 10x mid-session (observed after file picker)"}
old_value:
{"id":"ko2-tools-zlp","title":"TUI: app.suspend() for file picker degrades MIDI throughput 10x","description":"Confirmed from capture tui-2026-03-21-210118.jsonl:

Before yazi file picker (app.suspend()): download throughput 230-400 pages/s
After resume: throughput collapses to 24-37 pages/s (10x degradation)

This never recovers for the rest of the session. The degradation explains
why later waveform downloads take 60-98 seconds for small samples that 
should take 1-2 seconds.

Likely cause: app.suspend() disrupts the MIDI connection or CoreMIDI state. 
When the app resumes, the connection works but at massively reduced throughput.
The client never re-initializes or reconnects after resume.

Fix: either reconnect MIDI after app.suspend(), or call _initialize() on resume,
or avoid app.suspend() entirely (use in-app file picker instead).","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"ivan123.public@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-22T01:24:54Z","created_by":"Ivan Cherniukh","updated_at":"2026-03-22T01:24:54Z"}

old_value:
{"id":"ko2-tools-y64","title":"PROT-007: Document node_id dual-decode heuristic in PROTOCOL.md","description":"models.py:390-398 decode_node_id() silently tries both BE16 (hi<<8|lo) and U14 (hi<<7|lo) interpretations, using a name-prefix heuristic to decide which is correct. This dual-decode logic is undocumented in PROTOCOL.md.

The heuristic: if the entry name starts with 3 digits, it checks whether node_id_14-1000 or node_id_16-1000 matches that prefix. Falls back to 14-bit if in range 1000-12000, else 16-bit.

Risk: If the heuristic picks wrong, slot addressing silently fails (wrong slot operated on). This is especially dangerous for move/copy/delete where acting on the wrong slot is destructive.

Action items:
1. Document the dual-decode behavior in PROTOCOL.md with examples
2. Add captures/evidence for which encoding the device actually uses
3. Consider adding a validation step that warns when the two interpretations diverge
4. Log when heuristic fallback is used so failures are traceable

Evidence: src/core/models.py:390-398 (decode_node_id function)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"ivan123.public@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-03-21T19:01:01Z","created_by":"Ivan Cherniukh","updated_at":"2026-03-21T19:01:01Z"}
